title: educabR
text: Read English O educabR facilita o acesso dados educacionais brasileiros INEP, incluindo IDEB, ENEM e Censo Escolar.

title: educabR
text: Você pode instalar versão de desenvolvimento educabR pelo GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("SidneyBissoli/educabR")

title: educabR
code: library(educabR)  # Baixar IDEB 2021 - Anos Iniciais - Escolas ideb <- get_ideb(   year  = 2021,   stage = "anos_iniciais",   level = "escola" )

title: educabR
code: # Baixar microdados do ENEM 2023 enem <- get_enem(year = 2023)  # Resumo estatístico enem_summary(enem)

title: educabR
code: # Baixar Censo Escolar 2023 censo <- get_censo_escolar(year = 2023)

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Install from GitHub (development version) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("SidneyBissoli/educabR") library(educabR) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: educabR caches downloaded files avoid repeated downloads. default, uses temporary directory. persist data across sessions:
code: # Check current cache directory get_cache_dir()  # Set a permanent directory set_cache_dir("~/educabR_data")  # List cached files list_cache()  # Clear cache (when needed) clear_cache()

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: IDEB main indicator basic education quality Brazil, combining test performance (SAEB) grade promotion rates.

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # See available combinations list_ideb_available() #> # A tibble: 24 x 3 #>     year level     stage #>    <int> <chr>     <chr> #>  1  2017 escola    anos_iniciais #>  2  2017 escola    anos_finais #>  3  2017 escola    ensino_medio #>  4  2017 municipio anos_iniciais #>  ...

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # IDEB by school - Early elementary (1st-5th grade) ideb_schools <- get_ideb(   year = 2021,   level = "escola",   stage = "anos_iniciais" )  # IDEB by municipality - High school ideb_municipalities <- get_ideb(   year = 2023,   level = "municipio",   stage = "ensino_medio" )  # Filter by state (faster) ideb_sp <- get_ideb(   year = 2021,   level = "escola",   stage = "anos_iniciais",   uf = "SP" )

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # View structure glimpse(ideb_schools) #> Rows: 63,529 #> Columns: 17 #> $ sg_uf                   <chr> "RO", "RO", "RO", ... #> $ co_municipio            <dbl> 1100015, 1100015, ... #> $ no_municipio            <chr> "Alta Floresta D'Oeste", ... #> $ id_escola               <dbl> 11000023, 11000040, ... #> $ no_escola               <chr> "EEEE ABNAEL MACHADO DE LIMA", ... #> $ rede                    <chr> "Estadual", "Municipal", ... #> $ vl_aprovacao_2021_si_4  <dbl> 93.3, 98.5, 100, ... #> $ vl_indicador_rend_2021  <dbl> 0.92, 0.98, 1.00, ... #> $ vl_nota_matematica_2021 <dbl> 5.2, 5.8, 6.1, ... #> $ vl_nota_portugues_2021  <dbl> 5.4, 5.9, 6.0, ... #> $ vl_nota_media_2021      <dbl> 5.3, 5.85, 6.05, ... #> $ vl_observado_2021       <dbl> 4.9, 5.7, 6.1, ...

what: Example analysis: Average IDEB by state
title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Calculate average IDEB by state ideb_by_state <- ideb_schools |>   filter(!is.na(vl_observado_2021)) |>   group_by(sg_uf) |>   summarise(     n_schools = n(),     mean_ideb = mean(vl_observado_2021, na.rm = TRUE),     median_ideb = median(vl_observado_2021, na.rm = TRUE)   ) |>   arrange(desc(mean_ideb))  # Plot ggplot(ideb_by_state, aes(x = reorder(sg_uf, mean_ideb), y = mean_ideb)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Average IDEB by State - Early Elementary (2021)",     x = "State",     y = "Average IDEB"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Download historical series ideb_history <- get_ideb_series(   years = c(2017, 2019, 2021, 2023),   level = "municipio",   stage = "anos_iniciais" )  # National trend trend <- ideb_history |>   group_by(ano_ideb) |>   summarise(mean_ideb = mean(vl_observado, na.rm = TRUE))  ggplot(trend, aes(x = ano_ideb, y = mean_ideb)) +   geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1.2) +   geom_point(color = "darkgreen", size = 3) +   labs(     title = "IDEB Trend - Early Elementary",     x = "Year",     y = "National Average IDEB"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: ENEM Brazil’s largest exam, millions participants annually. microdata includes demographics, socioeconomic data, scores.

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # WARNING: Large files (1-3 GB)! # Use n_max for initial exploration  # Sample for exploration enem_sample <- get_enem(2023, n_max = 10000)  # Full data (takes a while!) # enem_full <- get_enem(2023)

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: glimpse(enem_sample) #> Rows: 10,000 #> Columns: 76 #> $ nu_inscricao      <dbl> 230001234567, ... #> $ nu_ano            <dbl> 2023, 2023, ... #> $ tp_faixa_etaria   <dbl> 3, 4, 2, ... #> $ tp_sexo           <chr> "F", "M", "F", ... #> $ tp_cor_raca       <dbl> 1, 3, 2, ... #> $ nu_nota_cn        <dbl> 512.3, 489.1, ... #> $ nu_nota_ch        <dbl> 598.2, 567.4, ... #> $ nu_nota_lc        <dbl> 534.8, 502.1, ... #> $ nu_nota_mt        <dbl> 478.9, 521.3, ... #> $ nu_nota_redacao   <dbl> 720, 640, ...

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Summary statistics for scores enem_summary(enem_sample)  # Summary by sex enem_summary(enem_sample, by = "tp_sexo")  # Average scores by race/ethnicity scores_by_race <- enem_sample |>   filter(!is.na(nu_nota_mt)) |>   mutate(     race = case_when(       tp_cor_raca == 0 ~ "Not declared",       tp_cor_raca == 1 ~ "White",       tp_cor_raca == 2 ~ "Black",       tp_cor_raca == 3 ~ "Mixed race",       tp_cor_raca == 4 ~ "Asian",       tp_cor_raca == 5 ~ "Indigenous"     )   ) |>   group_by(race) |>   summarise(     n = n(),     mean_math = mean(nu_nota_mt, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_essay = mean(nu_nota_redacao, na.rm = TRUE)   )

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Data about exam questions items <- get_enem_itens(2023)  glimpse(items) #> $ co_item        <dbl> 1, 2, 3, ... #> $ co_prova       <dbl> 1001, 1001, ... #> $ tp_lingua      <dbl> 0, 0, ... #> $ sg_area        <chr> "CN", "CN", ... #> $ co_habilidade  <dbl> 1, 2, ...

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: School Census Brazil’s main statistical survey basic education, covering public private schools.

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # School data schools_2023 <- get_censo_escolar(2023)  # Filter by state (faster) schools_sp <- get_censo_escolar(2023, uf = "SP")  # Sample for exploration schools_sample <- get_censo_escolar(2023, n_max = 1000)

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # The census contains over 400 variables per school! glimpse(schools_2023) #> Rows: 217,625 #> Columns: 408 #> $ nu_ano_censo     <dbl> 2023, 2023, ... #> $ sg_uf            <chr> "RO", "RO", ... #> $ co_uf            <dbl> 11, 11, ... #> $ no_municipio     <chr> "Porto Velho", ... #> $ co_municipio     <dbl> 1100205, ... #> $ no_entidade      <chr> "EEEE ABNAEL MACHADO DE LIMA", ... #> $ co_entidade      <dbl> 11000023, ... #> $ tp_dependencia   <dbl> 2, 3, 4, ... #> $ tp_localizacao   <dbl> 1, 1, 1, ...

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Count by administrative type schools_by_type <- schools_2023 |>   mutate(     admin_type = case_when(       tp_dependencia == 1 ~ "Federal",       tp_dependencia == 2 ~ "State",       tp_dependencia == 3 ~ "Municipal",       tp_dependencia == 4 ~ "Private"     )   ) |>   count(admin_type) |>   mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100)  ggplot(schools_by_type, aes(x = reorder(admin_type, n), y = n, fill = admin_type)) +   geom_col() +   geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), hjust = -0.1) +   coord_flip() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(     title = "Number of Schools by Administrative Type (2023)",     x = NULL,     y = "Number of Schools"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Check infrastructure availability in public schools infra <- schools_2023 |>   filter(tp_dependencia %in% c(2, 3)) |>  # Public schools only   summarise(     pct_internet = mean(in_internet == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     pct_library = mean(in_biblioteca == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     pct_computer_lab = mean(in_laboratorio_informatica == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     pct_sports_court = mean(in_quadra_esportes == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     pct_accessibility = mean(in_acessibilidade == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100   )  print(infra) #> # A tibble: 1 x 5 #>   pct_internet pct_library pct_computer_lab pct_sports_court pct_accessibility #>          <dbl>       <dbl>            <dbl>            <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1         78.3        42.1             35.2             48.7              32.1

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: Slow downloads: INEP servers can slow. package retries automatically 3 times. Large files: ENEM School Census files can several GB. Use n_max testing. Encoding: files encoding issues. package handles automatically.

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: Found bug want suggest improvements? Open issue GitHub: https://github.com/SidneyBissoli/educabR/issues

title: Introducao ao educabR
code: # Instalar do GitHub (versão de desenvolvimento) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("SidneyBissoli/educabR") library(educabR) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Introducao ao educabR
text: O educabR faz cache dos arquivos baixados para evitar downloads repetidos. Por padrão, usa um diretório temporário. Para persistir os dados entre sessões:
code: # Ver diretório de cache atual get_cache_dir()  # Configurar um diretório permanente set_cache_dir("~/educabR_data")  # Listar arquivos em cache list_cache()  # Limpar cache (quando necessário) clear_cache()

what: IDEB - Índice de Desenvolvimento da Educação Básica
title: Introducao ao educabR
text: O IDEB é o principal indicador de qualidade da educação básica Brasil, combinando desempenho em provas (SAEB) com taxas de aprovação.

what: ENEM - Exame Nacional do Ensino Médio
title: Introducao ao educabR
text: O ENEM é o maior exame Brasil, com milhões de participantes anualmente. Os microdados incluem informações demográficas, socioeconômicas e notas.

previous_headings: ENEM - Exame Nacional do Ensino Médio

text: O Censo Escolar é principal pesquisa estatística sobre educação básica Brasil, cobrindo todas escolas públicas e privadas.

title: Introducao ao educabR

title: Introducao ao educabR
text: Downloads lentos: Os servidores INEP podem ser lentos. O pacote tenta 3 vezes automaticamente. Arquivos grandes: ENEM e Censo Escolar podem ter vários GB. Use n_max para testar. Encoding: Alguns arquivos têm problemas de encoding. O pacote tenta corrigir automaticamente.

title: Plano de Expansão do educabR
text: O pacote educabR atualmente suporta 7 datasets INEP: Censo Escolar, ENEM, IDEB, SAEB, Censo da Educação Superior, ENADE e ENCCEJA. O objetivo é expandir para cobrir mais fontes de dados educacionais brasileiros, mantendo compatibilidade com o CRAN e seguindo os padrões já estabelecidos pacote. arquitetura atual é bem modular: cada dataset tem seu próprio R/get-{dataset}.R, e utilidades compartilhadas (utils-download.R, utils-cache.R, utils-validation.R) são reutilizáveis. Não serão necessárias novas dependências.

title: Plano de Expansão do educabR
text: Para cada dataset, os passos são idênticos: Verificar URL real navegador (HEAD request ou download manual) Baixar ZIP de 1 ano e inspecionar estrutura interna (nomes dos CSVs) Criar R/get-{dataset}.R seguindo o template de get_enem() Adicionar ao build_inep_url() e available_years() em utils-download.R Adicionar validação em utils-validation.R Criar testes (sem downloads reais — dados sintéticos) Rodar devtools::document() para gerar man pages Rodar devtools::check() localmente Atualizar NEWS.md, DESCRIPTION, _pkgdown.yml, vignettes Commit, push, verificar CI

title: Plano de Expansão do educabR
text: Checklist CRAN por submissão: - Exemplos com \dontrun{} - Cache em tempdir() por padrão - R CMD check: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes - cran-comments.md atualizado - Nenhuma dependência nova

title: Plano de Expansão do educabR
text: Para cada dataset implementado: 1. devtools::load_all() + get_dataset(year, n_max = 100) com cache limpo 2. Verificar encoding (acentos corretos) 3. Verificar tipos de colunas (numéricas como <dbl>, não <chr>) 4. Testar com pelo menos 2 anos diferentes (mais antigo e mais recente) 5. devtools::check() sem erros/warnings 6. CI verde GitHub Actions

what: educabR: Download and Process Brazilian Education Data from INEP — educabR-package
title: educabR: Download and Process Brazilian Education Data from INEP — educabR-package
text: Download process public education data INEP (Instituto Nacional de Estudos e Pesquisas Educacionais Anísio Teixeira). Provides functions access microdata School Census (Censo Escolar), ENEM (Exame Nacional Ensino Médio), SAEB (Sistema de Avaliação da Educação Básica), Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior), ENADE (Exame Nacional de Desempenho dos Estudantes), ENCCEJA (Exame Nacional para Certificação de Competências de Jovens e Adultos), IDD (Indicador de Diferença entre os Desempenhos Observado e Esperado), CPC (Conceito Preliminar de Curso), IGC (Índice Geral de Cursos), IDEB (Índice de Desenvolvimento da Educação Básica), educational datasets. Returns data tidy format ready analysis. Data source: INEP Open Data Portal https://www.gov.br/inep/pt-br/acesso--informacao/dados-abertos. educabR package provides functions download process public education data INEP (Instituto Nacional de Estudos e Pesquisas Educacionais Anísio Teixeira). offers easy access microdata : School Census (Censo Escolar): Annual data schools, enrollment, teachers, classes basic education ENEM: Data National High School Exam SAEB: Basic Education Assessment System (student performance) Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior): Annual data institutions, courses, students, faculty higher education ENADE: National Student Performance Exam (higher education) IDD: Value-added indicator undergraduate courses CPC: Preliminary Course Concept (undergraduate quality indicator) IGC: General Index Courses (institutional quality indicator) ENCCEJA: Youth Adult Education Certification Exam IDEB: Basic Education Development Index functions return data tidy format, ready analysis tidyverse tools.

title: Get Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior) data — get_censo_superior
text: Higher Education Census comprehensive statistical survey higher education institutions (HEIs) Brazil. collects data HEIs offering undergraduate graduate programs. Data types: "ies": One row per institution — administrative data, location, academic organization, funding type. "cursos": One row per undergraduate course — area study, modality (-person/distance), enrollment counts. "alunos": One row per student enrollment — demographics, program, admission type, enrollment status. "docentes": One row per faculty member — education level, employment type, teaching regime. Important notes: Student files ("alunos") can large (several GB). Use n_max read sample first. Column names standardized lowercase underscores. Use uf parameter filter state faster processing.

what: Censo da Educação Superior (Higher Education Census)
title: educabR 0.3.0
text: get_censo_superior(): Download Higher Education Census microdata (years 2009-2024). Supports multiple data types: institutions ("ies"), courses ("cursos"), students ("alunos"), faculty ("docentes"). list_censo_superior_files(): List available files downloaded census. UF filtering via uf parameter.

what: SAEB (Sistema de Avaliação da Educação Básica)
title: educabR 0.2.0
text: get_saeb(): Download SAEB microdata (years 2011, 2013, 2015, 2017, 2019, 2021, 2023). Supports multiple data types: student results ("aluno"), school ("escola"), principal ("diretor"), teacher ("professor") questionnaires. Handles SAEB 2021 special case INEP split downloads elementary/high school early childhood education files via level parameter.

title: educabR 0.2.0
text: Fixed encoding detection Windows using iconv() instead validEnc(). Fixed "Latin-1" encoding name "latin1" Windows codepage compatibility. Fixed ENEM 2024+ support: new type parameter split files ("participantes", "resultados"). Added SAS datetime parsing Censo Escolar date columns (dt_*). Converted IDEB vl_* columns character numeric, handling "-", "ND", comma decimals.

text: Added post-read data validation datasets. Errors empty corrupted files; warns expected columns missing (e.g., score columns ENEM, UF columns IDEB/Census) actionable messages. Downloads now show estimated file size starting (e.g., “downloading 2.3 GB INEP…”) via HTTP HEAD request, graceful fallback size unavailable. get_ideb_series() now shows per-year progress indication (e.g., “processing IDEB 2017 (1/4)”) propagates quiet parameter inner get_ideb() calls. get_enem_itens() now keep_zip parameter consistency get_enem() get_censo_escolar().
